steps.title: Extract two points from the graph to find the slope
steps.content: Reading from the graph, at $x = 0$ the line passes through $y = -2$, and at $x = -1$ the line passes through $y = 2$. The slope is $\frac{2 - (-2)}{-1 - 0} = \frac{4}{-1} = -4$.

steps.title: Write the equation of the graphed line
steps.content: Using the slope $m = -4$ and the $y$-intercept at $(0, -2)$, the equation of the line shown in the graph is $$y = -4x - 2$$

steps.title: Solve for f(x) using the given relationship
steps.content: The problem states the graph shows $y = f(x) - 11$. Substituting our equation: $$-4x - 2 = f(x) - 11$$ Adding $11$ to both sides: $$f(x) = -4x - 2 + 11 = -4x + 9$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
